_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use what we know about vowels, consonant blends and digraphs to read and write one- and two-syllable words.

_source.w: We are learning to use what we know about vowels, consonant blends and digraphs to read and write one- and two-syllable words.
y: Understanding sound patterns helps us decode new words we encounter in reading.
These phonics skills are the tools we use to unlock harder words.
The more patterns we learn, the more words we can read and spell independently.
eg: We read words with common long vowel sounds, like 'cake' and 'bike'.
We write words with consonant digraphs like 'sh', 'ch' and 'th'.
We break two-syllable words into parts and read each syllable.
We sort words by their vowel patterns — short 'a' versus long 'a'.
We use our phonics knowledge to attempt spelling new words in our writing.
We build words with magnetic letters, practising different consonant blends.
